tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Daily routines.
Part 1 (interview): Do you prefer mornings or evenings? How do you usually start your day? Has your daily routine changed in recent years?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a part of your day you especially enjoy. You should say: when it is, what you do, who you spend it with, and explain why you enjoy it.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do some people find routines comforting while others find them boring? How do work schedules affect people's health? Do you think flexible working hours will become more common?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——日常作息：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 个人陈述（1 分钟准备，至多 2 分钟）、Part 3 深入讨论。按文字转写作答估分。
referencePoints: Part 2 covers all cue points with organisation
topic vocabulary for routines and time management
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Places to live.
Part 1 (interview): Do you live in a house or a flat? What do you like about your neighbourhood? Is your hometown a good place for young people?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a town or city you would like to live in one day. You should say: where it is, how you first learned about it, what you would do there, and explain why it appeals to you.
Part 3 (discussion): What makes a city pleasant to live in? Should governments limit the size of big cities? How might cities change over the next fifty years?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——居住地：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述想居住的城市、Part 3 城市化讨论。
referencePoints: comparative and future forms in Part 3
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Learning skills.
Part 1 (interview): What skill are you learning at the moment? Do you prefer learning alone or with others? What was your favourite subject at school?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a skill that took you a long time to learn. You should say: what the skill is, why you started learning it, how you practised, and explain how you felt when you improved.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do adults sometimes find learning harder than children? Are online courses as effective as classroom learning? Which skills will be most valuable in the future?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——技能学习：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述花长时间掌握的技能、Part 3 学习方式讨论。
referencePoints: evaluated comparison of learning modes in Part 3
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Food and cooking.
Part 1 (interview): What did you eat this morning? Can you cook? Are eating habits in your country changing?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a meal you shared with friends or family that you remember well. You should say: what the occasion was, what you ate, who was there, and explain why you remember it.
Part 3 (discussion): Why is sharing food important in many cultures? Should schools teach students how to cook? How does advertising influence what people eat?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——饮食：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述难忘的一餐、Part 3 饮食文化讨论。
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Technology in everyday life.
Part 1 (interview): How often do you use your phone each day? What app do you find most useful? Is there any technology you try to avoid?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a piece of technology that has changed how you work or study. You should say: what it is, when you started using it, how you use it, and explain what difference it has made.
Part 3 (discussion): Do the benefits of smartphones outweigh the drawbacks? How has technology changed communication between generations? Should children's screen time be limited by law?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——科技生活：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述改变你工作/学习方式的科技、Part 3 科技影响讨论。
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Nature and the outdoors.
Part 1 (interview): Do you enjoy spending time outdoors? Is there much green space where you live? What was the last natural place you visited?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a natural place that impressed you. You should say: where it is, when you went there, what you saw and did, and explain why it impressed you.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do people living in cities need contact with nature? Who should be responsible for protecting natural areas — governments or individuals? Will tourism harm or help conservation in the long run?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——自然户外：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述让你印象深刻的自然景观、Part 3 自然保护讨论。
referencePoints: responsibility and policy discussion in Part 3
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Work and careers.
Part 1 (interview): What job would you like to do in the future? Is salary the most important thing about a job? Do people in your country often change careers?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a person whose job you admire. You should say: who they are, what they do, how you know about their work, and explain why you admire it.
Part 3 (discussion): What makes a job satisfying beyond money? How has the idea of a 'career for life' changed? Should companies be required to offer training to their employees?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——工作职业：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述你敬佩其职业的人、Part 3 职业发展讨论。
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Celebrations and traditions.
Part 1 (interview): What festivals are important in your country? How does your family celebrate special occasions? Do you prefer big celebrations or small ones?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a celebration you attended that you particularly enjoyed. You should say: what was being celebrated, where it took place, what happened, and explain why you enjoyed it.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do traditions matter to communities? Are traditional celebrations becoming too commercial? How do celebrations differ between generations?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——节庆传统：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述一次喜欢的庆祝活动、Part 3 传统文化讨论。
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Reading and media.
Part 1 (interview): Do you read more on paper or on a screen? What kind of stories do you enjoy? How do you usually get your news?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a book or article that changed the way you think about something. You should say: what it was, when you read it, what it was about, and explain how it changed your thinking.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do some people trust traditional news sources more than social media? Should reading fiction be encouraged in schools? Will printed books disappear in the future?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——阅读与媒体：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述改变你想法的书/文章、Part 3 媒体信任讨论。
referencePoints: abstract idea explained concretely in Part 2
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Health and exercise.
Part 1 (interview): How do you keep fit? Do you prefer indoor or outdoor exercise? Did you play any sports as a child?
Part 2 (long turn): Describe a healthy habit you would like to develop. You should say: what it is, why you want to develop it, what has stopped you so far, and explain how it would improve your life.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do many people find it hard to keep healthy habits? Should governments spend more on preventing illness than treating it? How can schools encourage children to be more active?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——健康运动：Part 1 访谈、Part 2 描述想养成的健康习惯、Part 3 公共健康讨论。
referencePoints: intention and obstacle language in Part 2
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2
